title: Electric Air Taxi Start-up Joby Aviation Surges in Trading on NYSE After SPAC Merger
text: Joby Aviation made its public trading debut on the New York Stock Exchange on August 11, 2021, after completing a merger with Reinvent Technology Partners, a special purpose acquisition company. The air taxi developer began trading on the NYSE under the ticker symbol JOBY after the merger closed. Reinvent Technology Partners is run by LinkedIn co-founder Reid Hoffman and Zynga founder Mark Pincus. The merger valued Joby at $6.6 billion. Shares surged more than 20 percent after opening at $10.62 in their first day of trading, indicating strong investor interest in the eVTOL sector. Joby obtained around $1.6 billion in total capital to take its air taxi operations to commercialization in 2024. Founded in 2009 by CEO JoeBen Bevirt, the start-up wants to make air travel cleaner and quieter. The company aims to bring its electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft into service in 2024. The SPAC merger marked a significant milestone for the electric air taxi industry, bringing Joby public just as the company ramped development and manufacturing of its S4 aircraft.
